metadata.title: Initialise the conversion of OpenClaude to .NET C#

subtasks.title: Bootstrap .NET solution structure and CI pipeline
subtasks.description: Create the initial .NET solution with project layout mirroring the TypeScript source structure. Set up the solution file, core project references, NuGet package manifest, and a GitHub Actions CI workflow that builds and runs unit tests. Establish coding conventions (EditorConfig, .gitignore additions, nullable reference types enabled).
acceptanceCriteria: `dotnet build dotnet/OpenClaude.sln` succeeds with zero errors and zero warnings on macOS and Windows (verified via CI matrix run).
`dotnet test` runs and passes the placeholder unit test in OpenClaude.Core.Tests (unit test — xUnit).
Nullable reference types (`<Nullable>enable</Nullable>`) and implicit usings are enabled globally via Directory.Build.props.
CI workflow triggers on push to `main` and on all PRs; both build and test steps are present.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — TDD the project structure validator (ensure projects reference each other correctly) before wiring up real code.

subtasks.title: Implement provider abstraction layer with Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible clients
subtasks.description: Port `src/services/api/providerConfig.ts` and surrounding provider logic to C#. Define `ILlmProvider` and `ILlmStreamingClient` interfaces. Implement `AnthropicProvider` using the Anthropic .NET SDK and `OpenAiCompatibleProvider` covering OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, Mistral, Ollama, and any other OpenAI-endpoint providers. Resolve provider type, base URL, model name, and API key from environment variables and profile JSON, matching the TypeScript provider detection logic.
acceptanceCriteria: `ProviderResolver.Resolve()` returns the correct provider type for each of: `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_OPENAI`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL`, and default Anthropic — verified by unit tests (xUnit, no live API calls).
`AnthropicProvider` and `OpenAiCompatibleProvider` both implement `ILlmProvider`; `dotnet build` succeeds with no warnings.
Provider config reads `OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_MODEL`, and `OPENAI_API_KEY` env vars, mirroring TypeScript `providerConfig.ts` logic — verified by unit tests with environment stubs.
Unsupported or missing provider config throws a typed `ProviderConfigException` with a descriptive message.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — mock HTTP responses; no live API calls in unit tests.

subtasks.title: Port core query engine with streaming tool loop and retry logic
subtasks.description: Port `src/query.ts` and `src/QueryEngine.ts` to C#. Implement the main query/response loop: send messages to the provider, process streamed tokens, detect and dispatch tool-use blocks, collect tool results, and feed them back. Implement exponential-backoff retry for transient errors. Map the TypeScript `QueryEngine` state machine to a C# async state machine using `IAsyncEnumerable<T>` for streaming.
acceptanceCriteria: `QueryEngine.RunAsync()` returns streamed text tokens via `IAsyncEnumerable<QueryChunk>` — verified by unit test with a stubbed provider returning a fixed multi-chunk response.

When the provider returns a tool-use block, `QueryEngine` calls `IToolExecutor.ExecuteAsync()`, appends the result to message history, and sends a follow-up request — verified by unit test with mock tool executor.

Transient HTTP errors (429, 503) trigger exponential-backoff retry up to the configured max attempts; quota-exhausted 429s are NOT retried — verified by unit tests (mirrors `fix(retry)` commit logic).
Message history is correctly trimmed when token count exceeds the configured context window limit.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first for retry/state machine logic; outside-in integration test for full tool loop with stub provider.

subtasks.title: Define tool system foundation: ITool interface, registry, and permission pipeline
subtasks.description: Port `src/Tool.ts` and the tool execution service to C#. Define `ITool`, `IToolExecutor`, and `ToolPermissionPolicy` interfaces. Implement `ToolRegistry` for tool discovery and lookup by name. Implement the permission pipeline: `ICanUseTool` callback, policy limits (rate limiting, quota), and streaming output via `IToolProgressSink`. This is the scaffold all built-in tools plug into.
acceptanceCriteria: `ToolRegistry.Register()` and `ToolRegistry.Resolve(name)` work correctly; duplicate registration throws `DuplicateToolException` — verified by unit tests.
When `ICanUseTool.IsAllowedAsync()` returns `false`, `IToolExecutor` does not call the tool and returns a `ToolResult` with `Denied` status — verified by unit test.
Streaming progress events are emitted to `IToolProgressSink` during execution — verified by unit test with a fake sink capturing events.
All interfaces are XML-documented with summary tags; `dotnet build` produces zero analyzer warnings.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — pure logic with no I/O; test registry, permission, and progress contracts in isolation.

subtasks.title: Implement built-in file and search tools (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep)
subtasks.description: Implement the five core file-system tools as `ITool` implementations: `FileReadTool`, `FileWriteTool`, `FileEditTool` (exact-string replacement), `GlobTool` (pattern matching via `Directory.GetFiles` and `Microsoft.Extensions.FileSystemGlobbing`), and `GrepTool` (content search, invoking system ripgrep or falling back to a managed implementation). Each tool validates inputs with Zod-equivalent schema (use `System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations` or FluentValidation).
acceptanceCriteria: `FileReadTool` returns correct content with line-number prefix for a fixture file — verified by unit test using a temp directory.
`FileEditTool` replaces the exact `old_string` with `new_string` and throws when `old_string` is not unique (or applies `replace_all`) — verified by unit tests.
`GlobTool` returns files matching `**/*.ts` in a fixture directory, sorted by modification time — verified by unit test.
`GrepTool` returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers for a regex pattern across a fixture directory — verified by unit test.
All tools return a structured `ToolResult` with `Content`, `IsError`, and `Metadata` fields.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — all tests use `Path.GetTempPath()` fixture directories; no external network calls.

subtasks.title: Implement built-in execution tools (Bash, PowerShell) with timeout and sandboxing
subtasks.description: Port `BashTool` and `PowerShellTool` from the TypeScript source. Use `System.Diagnostics.Process` to launch shell subprocesses. Implement configurable timeout, output streaming via `IToolProgressSink`, and a permission callback requiring explicit user approval before execution. On macOS/Linux use `/bin/bash`; on Windows use `powershell.exe` or `pwsh`. Capture stdout, stderr, and exit code in the `ToolResult`.
acceptanceCriteria: `BashTool.ExecuteAsync("echo hello")` returns stdout `hello` and exit code 0 — verified by integration test (runs real subprocess).
Execution exceeding the configured timeout is cancelled and returns a `ToolResult` with `IsError = true` and a timeout message — verified by unit test with a mocked `ProcessRunner`.
`BashTool` calls `ICanUseTool.IsAllowedAsync()` before launching the process; if denied, the process is never spawned — verified by unit test.
stdout and stderr are streamed incrementally to `IToolProgressSink` during long-running commands.
subtasks.testIntent: mixed — mock ProcessRunner for permission/timeout unit tests; real subprocess for integration smoke tests (skipped in CI sandbox).

subtasks.title: Build terminal UI foundation with Spectre.Console REPL loop
subtasks.description: Implement the interactive CLI entry point using Spectre.Console for rich terminal rendering (syntax highlighting, markdown-like formatting, progress spinners). Port the core REPL loop from `src/main.tsx`: read user input, dispatch to `QueryEngine`, stream response tokens to the terminal, and handle Ctrl+C gracefully. Implement the basic prompt, token-usage status line, and error display panels.
acceptanceCriteria: `dotnet run --project dotnet/src/OpenClaude.Cli -- --version` prints the version string and exits 0 (CLI E2E — manual verification).
The REPL loop starts, displays a prompt, sends a message to a stubbed `QueryEngine`, and streams the response tokens to the terminal — verified by a unit test with a captured `StringWriter` output.
Pressing Ctrl+C during streaming cancels the `CancellationToken` passed to `QueryEngine.RunAsync()` and prints a cancellation notice without throwing an unhandled exception.
Token usage (prompt tokens / completion tokens) is displayed in the status line after each response.
subtasks.testIntent: outside-in for REPL loop behaviour with stub engine; manual CLI E2E for terminal rendering correctness.

subtasks.title: Implement slash command system with core commands (/help, /config, /provider)
subtasks.description: Port the slash command registry from `src/commands.ts`. Define `ISlashCommand` with `Name`, `Description`, `ExecuteAsync`, and tab-completion metadata. Implement `CommandRouter` to parse `/command args` from REPL input and dispatch to the correct handler. Implement three foundational commands: `/help` (list commands), `/config` (show/edit settings JSON), and `/provider` (list and switch active provider profile).
acceptanceCriteria: `CommandRouter.ParseAndRoute("/help")` dispatches to `HelpCommand` and `CommandRouter.ParseAndRoute("/config show")` dispatches to `ConfigCommand` — verified by unit tests.
Unknown slash commands return a typed `CommandNotFoundResult` rather than throwing — verified by unit test.
`/help` lists all registered commands with descriptions; output is captured and verified in a unit test.
`/provider` lists available provider profiles from a fixture settings file — verified by unit test with a stub settings store.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — command dispatch logic is pure; use fake command stubs for routing tests.

subtasks.title: Implement configuration and settings management with profile support
subtasks.description: Port OpenClaude's profile and settings system. Implement `SettingsStore` to read/write `~/.openclaude/settings.json` and provider profiles (`.openclaude-profile.json`). Support environment-variable overrides matching the TypeScript env-var precedence order. Implement `ISecretStore` backed by the OS credential manager (macOS Keychain via `Security.framework` P/Invoke, Windows Credential Manager via `CredentialManagement` NuGet) for API key storage.
acceptanceCriteria: `SettingsStore.Load()` correctly merges environment-variable overrides on top of file-based config — verified by unit tests with isolated temp config files.
`EnvironmentSecretStore.GetSecret("OPENAI_API_KEY")` returns the env var value; platform-specific stores return `null` in unit tests (mocked platform check) — verified by unit tests.
Round-trip: write a profile to a temp file, reload it, and assert equality — verified by unit test.
Missing config file returns a default `ProfileConfig` with empty API key rather than throwing.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — all tests use `ISecretStore` fakes; no live keychain access in CI.

subtasks.title: Implement app state management with immutable store and reactive subscriptions
subtasks.description: Port `src/state/AppState.ts` and the state store to C#. Implement an immutable `AppState` record, `AppStateStore` (thread-safe, copy-on-write updates), and a reactive subscription API (`IObservable<AppState>` via `System.Reactive` or a lightweight custom implementation). Wire the store into `QueryEngine` and `ReplLoop` so state changes (e.g., active tool, token count, provider name) propagate to the status line.
acceptanceCriteria: `AppStateStore.Dispatch(action)` applies the action immutably and notifies all subscribers — verified by unit test with two fake subscribers.
Concurrent dispatches from two threads do not corrupt state — verified by a stress unit test (10 threads, 1000 dispatches each, final token count matches expected).
`StatusLine` subscribes to `AppStateStore` and re-renders when `ActiveTool` or `TokenCount` changes — verified by unit test with a captured `StringWriter`.
`AppState` is a C# `record` type ensuring value-equality; equality unit test passes.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first — pure state logic; concurrency test for thread-safety guarantee.

subtasks.title: Implement MCP (Model Context Protocol) client and tool discovery
subtasks.description: Port `src/services/mcp/` to C#. Implement `McpClient` that connects to MCP servers defined in `~/.claude/mcp-servers.json` (stdio and HTTP transports). Implement tool discovery: list tools from connected servers and register them dynamically into `ToolRegistry` as `McpProxyTool` instances. Handle server lifecycle (start, health-check, restart on crash).
acceptanceCriteria: `McpToolDiscovery.DiscoverAsync()` reads a fixture `mcp-servers.json` with two server entries and registers the correct number of `McpProxyTool` instances in `ToolRegistry` — verified by integration test with a fake MCP server process.
`McpProxyTool.ExecuteAsync()` forwards the call to the MCP server and returns the result — verified by unit test with a stubbed `McpClient`.
When an MCP server process exits unexpectedly, `McpServerLifecycle` attempts a restart up to the configured retry limit — verified by unit test with a mock process factory.
Servers not listed in `mcp-servers.json` are ignored; no exception is thrown.
subtasks.testIntent: mixed — unit tests for proxy/lifecycle logic; integration test with a real stdio echo server for discovery.

subtasks.title: Implement AgentTool, WebFetchTool, and WebSearchTool
subtasks.description: Port the three remaining high-value tools. `AgentTool` spawns a sub-`QueryEngine` with its own tool set and message history, returning the final response as a `ToolResult`. `WebFetchTool` fetches a URL via `HttpClient` and converts HTML to Markdown (use `HtmlAgilityPack` + `ReverseMarkdown`). `WebSearchTool` integrates with a configurable search provider (Brave Search API or SerpAPI) to return structured results.
acceptanceCriteria: `AgentTool.ExecuteAsync()` creates a child `QueryEngine`, runs it with the provided prompt, and returns the final text as `ToolResult.Content` — verified by unit test with a stubbed child engine returning a fixed response.
`WebFetchTool.ExecuteAsync(url)` returns Markdown-converted HTML content from a fixture HTML string — verified by unit test with a fake `HttpMessageHandler`.
`WebSearchTool.ExecuteAsync(query)` calls `IWebSearchProvider.SearchAsync()` and returns structured results with title, URL, and snippet — verified by unit test with a fake provider.
All three tools are registered in `ToolRegistry` at application startup — verified by an integration test that resolves each by name.
subtasks.testIntent: unit-first with fakes for HTTP and child engine; no live network calls in CI.
